_meta.note: Comparator labels are external reference labels only. They are NEVER BN training targets.

low.justification: Direct ordinal alignment: WOE low = BN low (both indicate minimal ecological concern)

negligible.justification: WOE negligible maps to BN low per governance spec Section 7.2 (both indicate no significant risk)

moderate.justification: Direct ordinal alignment: WOE moderate = BN moderate

moderate-to-high.justification: Conservative mapping per governance spec Section 7.2: WOE moderate-to-high maps to BN high

high.justification: Direct ordinal alignment: WOE high = BN high

externalSites.gateReason: WOE conclusions are site-level per receptor group, not station-level. PAH-only contamination profile (no metals COPCs). Cannot support station-level agreement statistics.
externalSites.reportTitle: Detailed Risk Assessment, Water Lot Portion of 1 Port Drive, Nanaimo, BC

bnInputCoverage.note: PAH-only contamination profile. 7 of 9 substance nodes (metals) have no site-specific evidence. BN inference is partial-evidence.

reportConclusions.woeIntegrationNote: Moderate chemistry hazard refuted by higher-weighted negligible toxicity and tissue LOEs.
reportConclusions.mappingJustification: Tetra Tech Negligible maps approximately to BN Low — both indicate minimal ecological concern. Mapping is approximate because the frameworks use different conceptual bases and the WOE scale is a 3-level hazard scale, not a 3-class ecological risk scale.

provenance.sourceDocument: Tetra Tech EBA Inc. (2015). Detailed Risk Assessment, Water Lot Portion of 1 Port Drive, Nanaimo, BC. File: ENVIND03511-02.

reportConclusions.mappingJustification: Aquatic macrophytes are not a BN-RRM receptor group. No mapping to 3-class ecological risk.

provenance.sourceDocument: Tetra Tech EBA Inc. (2015). Detailed Risk Assessment, Water Lot Portion of 1 Port Drive, Nanaimo, BC.

reportConclusions.mappingJustification: Fish risk derived from benthic results, not independently assessed. Not directly comparable to BN-RRM ecological_risk.

provenance.sourceDocument: Tetra Tech EBA Inc. (2015). Detailed Risk Assessment, Water Lot Portion of 1 Port Drive, Nanaimo, BC.

statisticalAuthorization.reason: No station-level paired labels. WOE conclusions are site-level per receptor. Only descriptive comparison authorized.
